title: Ternstroemia subsessilis
taxonomy: Ternstroemia subsessilis is classified within the family Ternstroemiaceae, a family of flowering plants in the order Ericales. This family is primarily composed of plants native to Asia and the Americas. The genus Ternstroemia belongs to the order Ericales, which also includes several other plant families such as the heath family (Ericaceae) and the blueberry family (Ericaceae). Ternstroemia subsessilis is a species within this genus, characterized by its unique characteristics and distribution. The classification of Ternstroemia subsessilis highlights the complex relationships within the plant kingdom.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ternstroemia subsessilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
